heading: Malign foreign talent recruitment program prohibition
label: 19232 Malign foreign talent recruitment program prohibition
notes: References in TextThe Civil Rights Act of 1964, referred to in subsec. (c), is , , . Title VI of the Act is classified generally to subchapter V (§ 2000d et seq.) of chapter 21 of this title. children.text: each covered individual listed in such proposal certify that each such individual is not a party to a malign foreign talent recruitment program in the proposal submission of each such individual and annually thereafter for the duration of the award; and

each institution of higher education or other organization applying for such an award certify that each covered individual who is employed by such institution of higher education or other organization has been made aware of the requirements under this section and complied with the requirement under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Stakeholder input
children.label: b Stakeholder input
children.text: In establishing a policy under subsection (a), Federal research agencies shall publish a description of the proposed policy in the Federal Register and provide an opportunity for submission of public comment for a period of not more than 60 days.

children.heading: Compliance with existing law
children.label: c Compliance with existing law
children.text: Each Federal research agency and recipient shall comply with title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.) in the establishment of policies pursuant to under subsection (a).42 U.S.C. 2000d1So in original.1
making scholarly presentations and publishing written materials regarding scientific information not otherwise controlled under current law;

participation in international conferences or other international exchanges, research projects or programs that involve open and reciprocal exchange of scientific information, and which are aimed at advancing international scientific understanding and not otherwise controlled under current law;
advising a foreign student enrolled at an institution of higher education or writing a recommendation for such a student, at such student’s request; and
other international activities determined appropriate by the Federal research agency head or designee.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: e Limitation
children.text: The certifications required under subsection (a) shall not apply retroactively to research and development awards made or applied for prior to the establishment of the policy by the Federal research agency.

children.heading: Training
children.label: f Training
children.text: Each Federal research agency shall ensure that, as a requirement of an award from each such agency, recipient institutions provide training on the risks of malign foreign talent recruitment programs to covered individuals employed at such institutions, including those individuals who are participating in activities described in subsection (d).
